title: K8s Node Storage & Pod Lifecycle
description: Pod lifecycle, per-pod ephemeral storage, node filesystem (/var deep dive), EmptyDir/PVC volume tracking, container I/O rates.

panels.title: Pods by Phase Over Time
panels.description: One query: sum by (phase) kube_pod_status_phase. Shows Running, Pending, Succeeded, Failed, Unknown all in one graph.

panels.title: Container Restart Rate per Pod
panels.description: Rate of container restarts. Spikes mean crash loops or OOMKills.

panels.title: All Pods — Start Time, Phase, Uptime
panels.description: Simple pod list. Start time, uptime, and phase from kube-state-metrics. No node filter since these metrics do not carry a node label.

panels.title: Ephemeral Storage per Pod — Stacked
panels.description: Each color = a pod. container_fs_usage_bytes tracks each container's writable layer and ephemeral writes (typically on /var). Height = total across all pods.

panels.title: Ephemeral Storage per Pod — Individual
panels.description: Each line = one pod. Compare against ephemeral-storage limits (dashed red).

panels.title: Top Pods by Ephemeral Storage
panels.description: Pods ranked by container filesystem usage. Shows usage, request, and limit.

panels.title: Ephemeral Storage by Container (writable layer)
panels.description: Per-container writable layer size. Each container writes to an overlay filesystem, this metric tracks that layer. Pod/container granularity.

panels.title: Node Filesystem — /var and All Mount Points
Filesystem Usage % — All Mount Points
panels.description: Each line = a mount point on any node. Shows how full each filesystem is. node_filesystem metrics come from node-exporter. If empty, node-exporter may not be installed or metrics may use 'instance' instead of 'node' label.

panels.title: /var Usage % Over Time
panels.description: Specifically tracks /var and /var/* mount points. /var is where Kubernetes stores container images, overlayfs layers, logs, and ephemeral volumes.

panels.title: Used Bytes Over Time — All Filesystems
panels.description: Absolute bytes used on each filesystem. Each line = one mount point.

panels.title: /var Used vs Total Capacity
panels.description: Red dashed = total /var capacity. Blue = used. Gap = free space.

panels.title: All Filesystems — Current Status
panels.description: Every filesystem reported by node-exporter. Shows device, mount point, FS type, size, used, available, and usage %. Uses 'instance' label for node identity.

panels.title: Volume Usage Over Time (EmptyDir + PVC)
panels.description: kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes tracks EmptyDir and PVC volumes mounted inside pods. EmptyDir volumes live on /var/lib/kubelet, consuming /var space.

panels.title: Volume Usage % (EmptyDir + PVC)
panels.description: How full each volume is. EmptyDir capacity = node ephemeral storage limit.

panels.title: All Volumes — Capacity, Used, Available
panels.description: EmptyDir and PVC volumes tracked by kubelet. Shows capacity, used, available.

panels.title: Container Write Rate (to ephemeral storage)
panels.description: Bytes/sec written by each container. High rates = actively filling /var.

panels.title: Container Read Rate (from ephemeral storage)
panels.description: Bytes/sec read by each container from its filesystem.
